reasoning: A has the most genuinely distinct five: Slosh turns the ball itself into a fluid-dynamics blob and Candlewake abandons moving bodies entirely for tending a zero-g flame, two ideas that escape the obvious 'push-off sport' frame in completely different conceptual spaces. C is close behind on craft and surprise, with Centrifuge (spin-restored gravity plus Coriolis-slanted jumps and a weightless axis) and Passant ('lending a teammate an up') as standouts, but its set sits in more conventional sport shapes than A's. B's five are competent but terse and partly clustered around the no-floor/push-off idea (No-Floor Siege, Collapse Choir), with sketch-level development rather than fully realized worlds, so it ranks last.
